source: NEURAL ARCHITECTURE SEARCH WITH REINFORCEMENT LEARNING .pdf

concepts.finding: An RNN controller trained with REINFORCE generates neural network architectures, using validation accuracy as reward signal, achieving 3.65% test error on CIFAR-10 and discovering recurrent cells outperforming LSTM on PTB.
concepts.implication: Automated architecture search via RL can discover architectures surpassing human-designed ones, suggesting NAS could optimize search-specific architectures like query encoders.
concepts.open_question: Can NAS discover better query encoder architectures than BERT-derived models for retrieval tasks?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: An RL-based NAS-discovered architecture for query encoding will achieve 3%+ higher NDCG@10 than BERT-base on search ranking while using 40% fewer parameters.
